segments.text: Hear my prayer, O Lord; let my cry come to you!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress! Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For my days pass away like smoke, and my bones burn like a furnace.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My heart is struck down like grass and has withered; I forget to eat my bread.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I am like a desert owl of the wilderness, like an owl of the waste places;
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I lie awake; I am like a lonely sparrow on the housetop.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My enemies taunt me all day long; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For I eat ashes like bread and mingle tears with my drink,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: because of your indignation and anger; for you have taken me up and thrown me down.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: But you, O Lord, are enthroned forever; you are remembered throughout all generations.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: You will arise and have pity on Zion; it is the time to favor her; the appointed time has come.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For your servants hold her stones dear and have pity on her dust.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Nations will fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth will fear your glory.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For the Lord builds up Zion; he appears in his glory;
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: he regards the prayer of the destitute and does not despise their prayer.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Let this be recorded for a generation to come,
so that a people yet to be created may praise the Lord:
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: that he looked down from his holy height; from heaven the Lord looked at the earth,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: to hear the groans of the prisoners, to set free those who were doomed to die,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: that they may declare in Zion the name of the Lord, and in Jerusalem his praise,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: when peoples gather together, and kingdoms, to worship the Lord.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: He has broken my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: O my God, I say, do not take me away in the midst of my days— you whose years continue throughout all generations!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Of old you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: They will perish, but you will remain; they will all wear out like a garment. You will change them like a robe, and they will pass away,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: but you are the same, and your years have no end.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The children of your servants shall dwell secure; their offspring shall be established before you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
